column2: As of August 1, 2024, are there any cities in England that are more populated than Philadelphia, and which cities are they?

Which event predates the other: the amalgamation of the Province of Betanzos with Mondonedo or the completion of a cathedral marking the destination of a prominent Spanish Catholic pilgrimage in the same province? Provide the year of the earlier event.
How many films with titles including the letter "g" did the director of "Sword of the Valiant" direct after the release of said film, but before the year 2000?
What medal did the woman who tied for 19th in the 2011 Pan American Games women's individual bowling event win as part of a women's bowling doubles team in the 2017 World Games?
Did the entomologist who first described lesticus purpurascens come from the same country as the entomologist who first described the subfamily of that species?

If you were to combine two words, the first of which has a species called Polytrichum piliferum, and the second which has a breed type called Fleckvieh, you'd get the name of what countries capital city?
What was the birthday of the man who was mayor of New York City the year Snoopy debuted in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade?
What is the average distance for the left field line in MLB stadiums with a retractable roof as of August 2024? Round to the nearest whole number.
As of August 3, 2024, which rabbi worked for both Reform Congregation Keneseth Israel in Philadelphia and Congregation Beth Israel in West Hartford, Connecticut?
Where was the Winter Olympics held the year that the girl who was Wheaties first official spokeswoman turned 20 years old?
How many of Hitler's three couriers of his last will and other political documents died after 1980?
The inventor of the first true pinhole camera was also the first to correctly explain what theory?
As of August 2024, the Atlanta Braves beat the Houston Astros the last time they won the World Series. How many years before this did Jackie Robinson join the Brooklyn Dodgers?
In which of the three Intertidal zones would you most likely find the Septifer bilocularis?
In the Eurovision Song Contest 2024, one country scored a combined total (jury and televoting results) of 268 - as did another country in the contest the year before. Which countries are they?
The quarterback who was selected first overall in the 1998 NFL draft, won the Superbowl with 2 different teams, both named after what type of animal?
As of 1st August 2024, How much younger is the current youngest US House Representative than the American folk hero who died at the Alamo when they were elected to the US House of Representatives?
Who was older, the guitar player for the Dugites from 1982-1983 or the lead singer of The Sports?
In the first movie that Emma Stone won an Academy Award for Best Actress in, did her costar win an Academy Award for Best Actor?

As of August 4, 2024, what is the first initial and surname of the cricketer who became the top-rated test batsman in the 2020s, is the fastest player of their country to 6 1000 run milestones in tests, and became their country's all-time leading run scorer in tests in the same year?
As of 2024, what percentage of Afroasiatic language speakers speak Central Atlas Tamazight?
The Terminator was released on October 26th exactly how many years after the famous gunfight at the O.K. Corral occurred?
If an Ixodes scapularis tick in its nymph stage feeds on a host in the Spring, how many seasons later is it most likely to transmit Lyme disease if it becomes an adult in the same year?

What percentage of his total league appearances did footballer Derek Smith (born 1946) make with the team whose original name is shared by a bird impressionist born in the nineteenth century? Give your answer to two decimal places.
Was the founder of the bank that was established 42 years before the National Banking Act was expanded to include the Territory of Hawai'i still alive when it acquired Pioneer Federal Savings Bank?

If you take the height of the Eiffel Tower in metres, add the number of arrondissements in Paris and subtract the street number of the official residence of the French prime minister, is the final answer a prime number?
Of the 3 largest canary islands, which has the hottest mean temperature in July as of 2024?
As of 1st August 2024, Are the actors who play Summer and Luke in the OC in anything else together?
How many years was the first vessel Stephen Etnier commanded constructed after the novel that first inspired him to pursue painting was published?
As of the financial year ending July 31st 2023, what was the size of the endowment at the university attended by rugby player Fred McLeod?
What medal was the captain of The RMS Titanic awarded by King Edward VII?
What painting was stolen from The Louvre exactly 56 years before the birth of activist and songwriter Serj Tankian?
As of August 1, 2024, which player who scored more than 10 goals in the 2022 Argentine Premier League season also played for Elche in Spain?
As of 2024, how many of the Star Wars actors whose first or last names are 'Jack' have starred in more than 2 Star wars movies?

I am thinking of a province that has the smallest land area in it's particular country, but also has the the 10th largest population.  This country has 10 provinces.  This province joined the country in 1873.  What is the scientific name of the provincial flower?
As of 1st January 2023, If I am 7 years younger than the eldest granddaughter of the female monarch with the longest reign in confirmed history was at the time of the monarch's death, how old am I?
The Office is an American mockumentary sitcom television series that first aired in 2005. Who won the Academy Award for Best Director the same year that the show had its series finale?
In square KM, how much bigger is the total area of La Casita-Garciasville, Texas compared to that of the independent state that was recognised in the 1929 Lateran Treaty?
Which fast food restaurant opened first, McDonald's, Wendy's or In-and-Out?
How much wider in centimeters is the painting that inspired a Stephen Sondheim musical than the 10th most expensive painting ever sold?
What is the birthplace and hometown of the winning goal scorer of the 2010 Vancouver Olympics, Men's Ice Hockey event?
Who won the World series the year Happy Days premiered?
How many days after the United States release of the record holder for largest sweep at the 2004 Oscars was the death of that movie's cinematographer?

In the Belgian capital, there is a street named after King Leopold II's oldest daughter which is lined with chestnut trees and is the home to many embassies. What is the capital of the country whose embassy is found at number 425?

I'm thinking of a man whose first name is Hart. He acted in a miniseries (based on a historical novel about WW2 with three words in its title) by an author who also wrote a book about a kid with the last name of Bookbinder.
Name the teams in alphabetical order that every AL MVP from Texas Rangers retired from as of August 2024.
What films did Big Hit Music's 7-member boy group release in the year that the company's 5-member boy group first debuted?
What year was the University that gave Taylor Swift an honorary doctorate founded?
What is the etymology of the name of the province to the east of the province in which Hazrati Sultan District is located?

Consider the number of months lapsing between the major earthquake that caused widespread destruction around the Bay of Naples in 62 CE to the eruption of Mount Vesuvius in 79 CE which buried Pompeii in ash.  If Mount Vesuvius were to have erupted once whenever that number of months came to pass between its 79 CE eruption and the date on which "Pompeii" by Bastille was officially released, how many times would the volcano have erupted between those two events?

Multiple the number of Tony's won by the guest host of SNL 12/6/1997 by the number of Oscar nominations received by the 2023 film directed by Greta Gerwig. Then divide this number by the number of Grammy's won by the band behind the 1979 album "Tusk".
On March 3rd during the year of Mariah Carey's birth, a famous space launch occurred and the mission lasted for how many days?
What is the Chinese name for the bodhisattva that the Sensoji temple is dedicated to?
Which president of the United States is represented by the sum of the ordinal numbers of the presidencies of the four men depicted on Mount Rushmore?
If we consider their inception being the date of declared independence, how many years older is the US than Mexico?
The manager of the Schenectady Blue Jays in 1953 also played Major League Baseball for which teams?
What is the name of the play written in May 2016 by a playwright who won the MacArthur Fellowship the same year as the poet who wrote "Postcolonial Love Poem"?
What is the birth date of the person picked right after Lee Vaughn in the 1997 NFL draft?

Suppose Egon Sendler's book "Les mystères du Christ: Icônes de la liturgie" was written in the same year as the birth of Nemanja Markovic. How old would the book be when the New Hampshire state election results for the Democratic party were 53.9% and 125,822 votes?
As of August 5, 2024, what is the name of the federal law that was found to be violated by the company that Brian Bergstein is employed by?
How many more votes did the Conservatives receive in the Highlands and Islands region in the 2021 Scottish Parliamentary Elections than in 2016?
What lake in Isreal supports a population of the state bird of the Indian state Haryana?
As of August 3, 2024, what is the sum of the birth years of every tennis player to both represent the country that tennis was first played and complete a Grand Slam.
Rosie Ruiz was disqualified from the Boston Marathon. The subsequent winner placed in what position of the 1988 Grandma's Marathon?

On Nov 15, 2017 a painting was sold for US $450 million setting a new record for the most expensive painting ever sold at public auction. What year was the auction house where this purchase took place founded?
A disease that had millions of dollars raised for on April 20, 1992, was first recognized by the Center for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) in what year?
As of July 1, 2023, what is the total number of letters in the names of the capital cities of the 5 most populated countries in the world?
Where was the rapper behind the song "Hind's Hall" born?

Based on the information available on Wikipedia on August 4, 2024 at 2:42 AM Greenwich Mean Time,  which of the following areas, Minamidaitōjima, Nuapada district, or Vostochnaya Niva has the highest population and how many more citizens does it contain than the other two provided cities?
What are the first three letters of the capital city of the country where Shakespeare's longest play is set?
How many of Ron Hutchinson's teams won League Championships while he was on them?
What Pink Floyd album came out the year Pablo Picasso died?
As of August 1 2024, what books has the author of the Harry Potter series written under an alias?
What football team did Travis Kelce play for the year Taylor Swift's VMA acceptance speech was interrupted by Kanye West?
What horror movie remake did the director who was the first to attempt and failed to make Brokeback Mountian into a film direct in the 90's?
As of 2024, how many total Academy award nominations has the the man who won the Academy award for best actor one year before 1999 received?
What is the difference between the fastest recorded swimming speed of a fish, and the fastest record for swimming the 50m freestyle in the 2020 Tokyo Olympics in meters per second?
How many years separate the birth of Alexander Graham Bell and the birth of Charles Dickens?

Stamatios Krimigis was named after an asteroid by the IAU. The observing site that found this asteroid is part of a large observatory known for discovering a planet. What is the difference in years between the discovery of this asteroid and the discovery of the planet?

How many years after Ghengis Khan died did World War II begin?
Among the singers in the 1985 version of "That's What Friends Are For," which one was born in the Peach State?
On what station did the television show that started in 1993 and had a star who shared a name with the third wife of King Henry VIII first run?
